title: Chip Industry Technical Paper Roundup: June 30
gist: A roundup of newly added semiconductor research papers spanning DRAM in-memory-compute reliability, atomic-scale plasma processing, gallium oxide defects, RL-based fab control, embedded-processor side channels, LLM-generated RTL, and Google's five-generation TPU supercomputer architecture.

insights.claim: Processing-using-DRAM operations executed on real DRAM chips can suffer computation-result corruption, raising reliability implications for future in-memory-compute systems.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Signals that processing-in-memory, a touted memory-wall workaround, has unresolved silicon-level correctness risks worth tracking before betting on PIM startups or roadmaps.
insights.quote: PuDGhost: Experimental Analysis of Computation Result Corruption in Processing-using-DRAM Operations on Real DRAM Chips and Implications for Future Systems

insights.claim: Research is advancing atomic-scale controlled plasma processing, a fabrication-precision technique developed across an international consortium including Nagoya University, Hitachi High-Tech, and Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Atomic-scale etch/deposition control is a gating capability for sub-2nm nodes; watch equipment vendors like Hitachi High-Tech commercializing it.
insights.locator: Recent Progress in Atomic-Scale Controlled Plasma Processing

insights.claim: Si-doped gallium oxide exhibits defect-driven phase transformation and instability, a coordination-sensitive nanoscale problem studied by University at Buffalo, Ohio State, and Lawrence Livermore.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: Phase instability in doped Ga2O3 is a risk factor for the wide-bandgap power-semiconductor thesis; relevant to power-device material bets.

themes.theme: AI in fab and design flows

insights.claim: Event-driven reinforcement learning has been shown to enable long-horizon control in semiconductor fabrication, in work co-authored by STMicroelectronics and Politecnico di Milano.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: IDM involvement (STMicro) signals RL-based process control is moving from academia toward real fab deployment; watch fab-automation and APC tooling.
insights.quote: Event-Driven Reinforcement Learning Enables Long-Horizon Control in Semiconductor Fabrication

insights.claim: LLMs can be tool-assisted to generate RTL (register-transfer-level hardware code), per LLM4RTL work from UC Riverside and Futurewei.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: LLM-driven RTL generation threatens to compress front-end design labor; a structural watch item for EDA incumbents (Synopsys, Cadence) and design-services margins.
insights.quote: LLM4RTL: Tool-Assisted LLM for RTL Generation

insights.claim: MIPSBLEED research uncovered microarchitectural timing leaks in pervasive embedded processors, a hardware side-channel vulnerability class.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: New side-channel classes in widely deployed embedded cores create patch/redesign liabilities relevant to IoT and edge-silicon security exposure.
insights.quote: MIPSBLEED: Uncovering Microarchitectural Timing Leaks in Pervasive Embedded Processors

insights.claim: Google, with UC Berkeley, documented architectural stability, scale, resilience, power efficiency, and sustainability of its TPU training supercomputers across five generations from TPU v2 to Ironwood.
insights.speaker: Linda Christensen
insights.so_what: A rare first-party account of five TPU generations gives insight into Google's custom-silicon durability and power-efficiency edge versus merchant GPUs.
insights.quote: Google's Training Supercomputers from TPU v2 to Ironwood: Architectural Stability, Scale, Resilience, Power Efficiency, and Sustainability Across Five Generations
